title: rosa canina var. cabradensis
intro: Rosa canina var. cabradensis, also known as the Cabradensis Rose or Wild Rose, is a shrub species within the Rosa canina species, native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. cabradensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune system function. The plant is typically harvested for its hips, which are then used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs for enhanced medicinal effects.
